petition2: black makes about 5 points of solid territory, has a big endgame move to capture at s4, and most importantly, the position is unassailable -- black has a free hand to play elsewhere, attack the pincer stone, or play moves against the white formation

petition2: white, on the ohter hand, has a framework of about ten points, but none of it is solid territory. on this board, black r13 would be a big forcing move, as the aji of a splitting attack at r9 is aggravated by the solid black position below
